description: SSA Import Schema — defines how ALL inbound imports (recipe, cleaning, garden/seed, animal/butchery, storehouse, video/how-to, future domains) are stored, normalized, enriched into context intelligence, and linked to automation. This schema is the bridge for: imports → intelligence → automation → (optional) hub export.
notes: This file is intended to be read by src/db/index.js OR by src/features/import/ImportService.js to know what shapes to persist.
Imports *do not* own the household — they are inputs. But every import should be storable and replayable.
Every import should produce or update a record in the main household DB (see schema.household.json → 'imports'). This file gives a more detailed, domain-aware schema specifically for imports.
Keep timestamps as ISO strings so the eventBus payload shape { type, ts, source, data } can be mirrored here.
When an import is successfully parsed/normalized, emit event: { type: 'import.parsed', ts: <ISO>, source: 'import.service', data: { id, householdId, domain, source, normalizedPayload } }.
If familyFundMode=true (see featureFlags in household schema), the import service may format and enqueue this import for Hub export.

importSources.description: Registry of known import sources (domains and hosts). Used to check the siteAllowList and map to the right normalizer.

importQueue.description: Staging area for imported payloads BEFORE full normalization. This is where bookmarklet, share target, iOS Shortcut POST, file upload, or Scan • Compare • Trust dumps its data. ImportService drains this queue.

imports.description: Canonical import records AFTER normalization. Every domain (recipe, cleaning, garden/seed, animal/butchery, storehouse, video/how-to, future preservation) ends up here.

importErrors.description: Any failed import parsing/normalization attempts. Helps tune the siteAllowList and mappers.

importMappings.description: Mapper definitions for how to turn an import domain → SSA internal shape. Think: recipe JSON → mealPlanEngine, cleaning blog → CleaningSessionEngine, seed packet → GardenSessionEngine.

importToSessionRequests.description: Requests created by the ImportService or the user saying: 'turn this import into an actionable session'. Automation/runtime consumes this and writes to household.sessions (see schema.household.json).

importExports.description: Records of what imports were exported to the Hub (only if familyFundMode=true). This lets us avoid re-sending unchanged imports.

recipe.description: Recipe/meal-related imports. Should produce ingredients, methods, equipment, seasonality → link to inventory/storehouse → suggest cooking session.

cleaning.description: Cleaning/decluttering/zone routine imports. Should produce taskGraph and room/zone metadata → suggest cleaning session.

garden.description: Garden/seed imports. Should produce crops, zones, care schedule, and harvest targets → suggest garden session and link to storehouse goals.

animal.description: Animal acquisition, care, and butchery imports. Should produce careSchedule, species, yieldCurveRef → suggest animal session.

storehouse.description: Storehouse planning imports. Should produce target stock levels → link to inventory + automation for resupply and production.

video.description: Video/how-to imports. Should extract taskGraph/equipment if possible, and allow user to request a session based on the video.
